name: wireSize
invariants: output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || typeof output.result.recommendedWire === 'string'
output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || typeof output.result.minBreaker === 'string'
output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || typeof output.result.upsizedForVoltageDrop === 'boolean'
output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || typeof output.result.basis === 'string'
_note: Pure compute. NecCalculators sends lensRun('electrical','wireSize',{loadAmps,continuous,distanceFeet,voltage}) → body.input flat → handler reads artifact.data (== params). designAmps = load × (continuous ? 1.25 : 1); ampacity wire per NEC 310.16 @ 75°C copper; then walks UP gauges until voltage drop ≤ 3%. Fail-closed: loadAmps sanitises via num() (non-finite/negative → 0) and a non-positive load returns the honest 'Enter the circuit load' prompt — a poisoned load NEVER fabricates a wire/breaker recommendation (the dangerous failure mode where garbage prints a confident 4/0 spec). Cards render recommendedWire/minBreaker/voltageDropAtRecommended/designAmps/ampacityRequiredWire/recommendedAmpacity/upsizedForVoltageDrop/basis EXACTLY (field-aligned against WireSizeCalc). No STATE, no DB.
